prompt:
We run a Mastermind ladder in the team channel and I keep losing, so I want a reference SOLVER I can study — not a scorer, a thing that cracks codes. PowerShell, one script `mmsolver.ps1`, run as `pwsh -NoProfile -File mmsolver.ps1 -Secret <code>`. Set-StrictMode-clean (Latest), ordinal string comparisons, plain Write-Output lines only (no Format-* anywhere near the output).

Codes are 4 digits, each 1-6, repeats allowed. Anything else handed to -Secret (or nothing at all) -> `error: secret must be 4 digits 1-6` on stderr, exit 2, nothing on stdout.

The solver plays both sides: it knows the secret only through the feedback pegs it computes for each of its own guesses. Feedback law (spelling it out so we argue about it exactly once): BLACK = positions where guess and secret agree. WHITE = over the six digit values, sum the smaller of (occurrences in secret) and (occurrences in guess), then subtract black. So secret 1122 vs guess 2211 -> black=0 white=4; secret 1234 vs guess 1111 -> black=1 white=0.

Strategy, pinned exactly — consistency filtering: start from the full candidate list, all 1296 codes in lexicographic order 1111..6666. The FIRST guess is always `1122`. After each guess, keep only the candidates that would have produced the identical black/white feedback had they been the secret. The next guess is always the lexicographically FIRST surviving candidate. No minimax, no cleverness — I want the classic simple strategy, reproducible run to run.

Output, one line per guess: `GUESS <n> <guess> black=<b> white=<w> candidates=<k>` where k is the surviving-candidate count AFTER filtering on that guess's feedback. When the guess hits black=4, follow with `CRACKED <secret> in <n> guesses` (`in 1 guess` when the opener lands) and exit 0.

`fixtures/secrets.txt` holds ladder secrets with the guess counts this strategy must reproduce. `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_mmsolver.ps1` is the acceptance gate — it pins whole transcripts (those candidate counts are the strategy's fingerprint, they must match digit for digit). Don't touch the test file or fixtures.
